assessment_items.question_content:
Which artist relied on a "paranoiac-critical method," based loosely on the ideas of Sigmund Freud, to generate imagery in painting? 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2d4b257ddf8bd11a065c4c3069ff0eda.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Dali believed that this method gave him access to the conscious and unconscious mind simultaneously and allowed him to create dream-like images that could be understood in more than one way.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Salvador Dalí described some of the Surrealist objects as “objects with a literal function.” These items were meant to be used, despite their unusual appearance.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e8839bc0a9774d12b25fd10d270ba324.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Salvador Dalí described some of the Surrealist objects as “objects with a symbolic function.” An ordinary, functional object was modified to create a new object that was both personal and poetic.** 

**[Read this essay to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1907-1960-age-of-global-conflict/dada/a/meret-oppenheim-object-fur-covered-cup-saucer-and-spoon-1936)**

**Meret Oppenheim's *Object* recalls the Surrealists' love of alchemical transformation by turning cool, smooth ceramic and metal into something warm and bristley.**

**[Read this essay to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1907-1960-age-of-global-conflict/dada/a/meret-oppenheim-object-fur-covered-cup-saucer-and-spoon-1936)**

choices.content: the Surrealists' interest in propriety and order.
the Surrealists' love of alchemical transformation.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What detail of this painting best demonstrates Dalí's interest in accessing both the unconscious and conscious minds simultaneously?**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2d4b257ddf8bd11a065c4c3069ff0eda.jpeg)

Salvador Dalí, *Metamorphosis of Narcissus*, 1937, oil on canvas, 511 x 781 cm (Tate)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The doubling of forms in this painting demonstrate how Dali created images that could be read in multiple ways.

choices.content: the doubled forms in the foreground
the flower growing from the egg
the mythological references in the landscape

assessment_items.question_content:
**What interest among Surrealists is suggested by Giacometti's subject matter in *The Palace at 4am*?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5e001cebd7f3319237f3e2772c8230e1.jpeg)

Alberto Giacometti, *Palace at 4am*, wood, glass, wire and string, 1932 (MoMA)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Giacometti's sculpture represents a tableau or performance that takes place at night in the artist's subconscious.
